papers.title: UniVidX: A Unified Multimodal Framework for Versatile Video Generation via Diffusion Priors
papers.abstract: Abstract UniVidX is a unified multimodal framework that uses video diffusion model priors for versatile video generation through stochastic condition masking, decoupled gated LoRA, and cross-modal self-attention mechanisms.  AI-generated summary Recent progress has shown that video diffusion models (VDMs) can be repurposed for diverse multimodal graphics tasks. However, existing methods often train separate models for each problem setting, which fixes the input-output mapping and limits the modeling of correlations across modalities. We present UniVidX, a unified multimodal framework that leverages VDM priors for versatile video generation. UniVidX formulates pixel-aligned tasks as conditional generation in a shared multimodal space, adapts to modality-specific distributions while preserving the backbone's native priors, and promotes cross-modal consistency during synthesis. It is built on three key designs. Stochastic Condition Masking (SCM) randomly partitions modalities into clean conditions and noisy targets during training, enabling omni-directional conditional generation instead of fixed mappings. Decoupled Gated LoRA (DGL) introduces per-modality LoRAs that are activated when a modality serves as the generation target, preserving the strong priors of the VDM. Cross-Modal Self-Attention (CMSA) shares keys and values across modalities while keeping modality-specific queries, facilitating information exchange and inter-modal alignment. We instantiate UniVidX in two domains: UniVid-Intrinsic, for RGB videos and intrinsic maps including albedo, irradiance, and normal; and UniVid-Alpha, for blended RGB videos and their constituent RGBA layers. Experiments show that both models achieve performance competitive with state-of-the-art methods across distinct tasks and generalize robustly to in-the-wild scenarios, even when trained on fewer than 1,000 videos. Project page: https://houyuanchen111.github.io/UniVidX.github.io/

ru.title: Единая видеогенерирующая модель для всех мультимодальных задач
ru.desc: UniVidX — это единая мультимодальная архитектура, которая переиспользует приоры видео-диффузионных моделей для универсального видеогенерирования. Ключевые инновации включают стохастическое маскирование условий (SCM), позволяющее переключаться между модальностями при обучении, и развязанную LoRA с вентилями для сохранения сильных приоров исходной модели. Механизм кросс-модального самовнимания (CMSA) обеспечивает информационный обмен между модальностями через общие ключи и значения. Система демонстрирует конкурентные результаты на различных задачах видеосинтеза, включая восстановление внутренних характеристик и слоёв альфа-канала.

en.title: UniVidX: Unifying Video Generation with Multimodal Intelligence
en.desc: UniVidX is a novel multimodal framework that enhances video generation by utilizing video diffusion model (VDM) priors. It addresses the limitations of existing methods that require separate models for different tasks by enabling conditional generation in a shared multimodal space. The framework incorporates Stochastic Condition Masking to allow flexible input-output mappings, Decoupled Gated LoRA to maintain strong model priors, and Cross-Modal Self-Attention for effective information sharing across modalities. Experiments demonstrate that UniVidX performs competitively with state-of-the-art techniques, even with limited training data.

zh.title: 统一多模态框架，视频生成新突破
zh.desc: UniVidX 是一个统一的多模态框架，利用视频扩散模型的先验知识进行多样化的视频生成。它通过随机条件掩蔽、解耦门控 LoRA 和跨模态自注意力机制来实现这一目标。与传统方法不同，UniVidX 在共享的多模态空间中将像素对齐任务视为条件生成，从而增强了模态间的一致性。实验表明，UniVidX 在不同任务中表现出色，并能在少于 1000 个视频的训练下，仍然具有良好的泛化能力。

papers.title: Web2BigTable: A Bi-Level Multi-Agent LLM System for Internet-Scale Information Search and Extraction
papers.abstract: Abstract Web2BigTable is a multi-agent framework that addresses both broad and deep web search challenges through a bi-level architecture with coordinated agents and iterative improvement mechanisms.  AI-generated summary Agentic web search increasingly faces two distinct demands: deep reasoning over a single target, and structured aggregation across many entities and heterogeneous sources. Current systems struggle on both fronts. Breadth-oriented tasks demand schema-aligned outputs with wide coverage and cross-entity consistency, while depth-oriented tasks require coherent reasoning over long, branching search trajectories. We introduce Web2BigTable, a multi-agent framework for web-to-table search that supports both regimes. Web2BigTable adopts a bi-level architecture in which an upper-level orchestrator decomposes the task into sub-problems and lower-level worker agents solve them in parallel. Through a closed-loop run--verify--reflect process, the framework jointly improves decomposition and execution over time via persistent, human-readable external memory, with self-evolving updates to each single-agent. During execution, workers coordinate through a shared workspace that makes partial findings visible, allowing them to reduce redundant exploration, reconcile conflicting evidence, and adapt to emerging coverage gaps. Web2BigTable sets a new state of the art on WideSearch, reaching an Avg@4 Success Rate of 38.50 (7.5times the second best at 5.10), Row F1 of 63.53 (+25.03 over the second best), and Item F1 of 80.12 (+14.42 over the second best). It also generalises to depth-oriented search on XBench-DeepSearch, achieving 73.0 accuracy. Code is available at https://github.com/web2bigtable/web2bigtable.

ru.title: Многоагентный поиск в интернете с координацией и итеративным совершенствованием
ru.desc: Web2BigTable — это многоагентный фреймворк, который решает задачи поиска информации в интернете как в ширину, так и в глубину благодаря двухуровневой архитектуре. Система использует оркестратор верхнего уровня для декомпозиции задач и параллельно работающих агентов нижнего уровня для их решения. Через итеративный цикл проверки и рефлексии фреймворк улучшает как декомпозицию задач, так и их исполнение, сохраняя знания в явной памяти. Агенты координируются через общее рабочее пространство, что позволяет им снижать избыточный поиск, согласовывать противоречивые данные и адаптироваться к пробелам в покрытии информации.

en.title: Revolutionizing Web Search with Multi-Agent Coordination
en.desc: Web2BigTable is a multi-agent framework designed to tackle the challenges of web search by using a bi-level architecture. It effectively manages both broad search tasks, which require wide coverage and consistency, and deep reasoning tasks that involve complex, long search paths. The framework employs an orchestrator to break down tasks into smaller parts, which are then handled by worker agents that collaborate and share findings to minimize redundancy. This innovative approach not only improves search efficiency but also sets new performance benchmarks in web-to-table search tasks.

zh.title: Web2BigTable：多智能体框架的网络搜索新突破
zh.desc: Web2BigTable是一个多智能体框架，旨在解决广泛和深入的网络搜索挑战。它采用双层架构，上层协调器将任务分解为子问题，下层工作智能体并行解决这些问题。该框架通过闭环的运行-验证-反思过程，利用持久的可读外部记忆不断改进任务分解和执行。Web2BigTable在WideSearch上设定了新的最佳状态，并在深度搜索任务中也表现出色。

papers.title: Map2World: Segment Map Conditioned Text to 3D World Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Map2World enables 3D world generation from user-defined segment maps with improved scale consistency and detail enhancement through a pipeline leveraging asset generator priors.  AI-generated summary 3D world generation is essential for applications such as immersive content creation or autonomous driving simulation. Recent advances in 3D world generation have shown promising results; however, these methods are constrained by grid layouts and suffer from inconsistencies in object scale throughout the entire world. In this work, we introduce a novel framework, Map2World, that first enables 3D world generation conditioned on user-defined segment maps of arbitrary shapes and scales, ensuring global-scale consistency and flexibility across expansive environments. To further enhance the quality, we propose a detail enhancer network that generates fine details of the world. The detail enhancer enables the addition of fine-grained details without compromising overall scene coherence by incorporating global structure information. We design the entire pipeline to leverage strong priors from asset generators, achieving robust generalization across diverse domains, even under limited training data for scene generation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms existing approaches in user-controllability, scale consistency, and content coherence, enabling users to generate 3D worlds under more complex conditions.

ru.title: От карты к миру: контролируемая генерация 3D-сцен с согласованным масштабом
ru.desc: Map2World — это фреймворк для генерации трёхмерных сцен на основе пользовательских карт сегментации произвольной формы и масштаба. Метод решает проблему несогласованности масштабов объектов во всей сцене благодаря глобальной нормализации и специальной архитектуре. Авторы предлагают сеть-усилитель деталей, которая добавляет мелкие детали, сохраняя при этом общую согласованность сцены через использование глобальной структурной информации. Подход использует сильные приоры от генераторов ассетов, обеспечивая хорошую обобщаемость на разнообразные домены даже при ограниченных данных для обучения.

en.title: Create Consistent and Detailed 3D Worlds with Map2World!
en.desc: Map2World is a framework that allows users to create 3D worlds from custom segment maps, improving the consistency of object scales and enhancing details. It addresses limitations of previous methods that relied on grid layouts, which often led to inconsistencies in the generated environments. The framework includes a detail enhancer network that adds fine details while maintaining the overall coherence of the scene. By utilizing strong priors from asset generators, Map2World achieves better generalization across various domains, even with limited training data, making it easier for users to create complex 3D worlds.

zh.title: 用户定义的 3D 世界生成新方法
zh.desc: Map2World 是一种新颖的 3D 世界生成框架，能够根据用户定义的分段地图生成任意形状和规模的 3D 世界。该方法通过引入细节增强网络，提升了生成世界的细节质量，同时保持了整体场景的一致性。与传统方法相比，Map2World 在用户可控性、规模一致性和内容连贯性方面表现出显著优势。该框架适用于沉浸式内容创作和自动驾驶模拟等多种应用场景。

papers.title: Prox-E: Fine-Grained 3D Shape Editing via Primitive-Based Abstractions
papers.abstract: Abstract A training-free framework for fine-grained 3D editing that uses geometric primitives and vision-language models to preserve identity while enabling localized structural changes.  AI-generated summary Text-based 2D image editing models have recently reached an impressive level of maturity, motivating a growing body of work that heavily depends on these models to drive 3D edits. While effective for appearance-based modifications, such 2D-centric 3D editing pipelines often struggle with fine-grained 3D editing, where localized structural changes must be applied while strictly preserving an object's overall identity. To address this limitation, we propose Prox-E, a training-free framework that enables fine-grained 3D control through an explicit, primitive-based geometric abstraction. Our framework first abstracts an input 3D shape into a compact set of geometric primitives. A pretrained vision-language model (VLM) then edits this abstraction to specify primitive-level changes. These structural edits are subsequently used to guide a 3D generative model, enabling fine-grained, localized modifications while preserving unchanged regions of the original shape. Through extensive experiments, we demonstrate that our method consistently balances identity preservation, shape quality, and instruction fidelity more effectively than various existing approaches, including 2D-based 3D editors and training-based methods.

ru.title: Точное редактирование 3D без обучения через геометрические примитивы
ru.desc: В статье представлена Prox-E — фреймворк без обучения для точного редактирования 3D-моделей, использующий геометрические примитивы и предобученные vision-language модели. Метод работает путём абстрагирования входной 3D-формы в компактный набор геометрических примитивов, которые затем редактируются через VLM на основе текстовых команд. Полученные структурные изменения на уровне примитивов направляют работу генеративной модели, позволяя применять локализованные модификации при сохранении неизменённых областей исходной формы. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что Prox-E лучше балансирует сохранение идентичности объекта, качество геометрии и точность следования инструкциям по сравнению с существующими 2D-центричными и обучаемыми методами.

en.title: Fine-Grained 3D Editing Made Easy with Prox-E
en.desc: This paper presents Prox-E, a novel framework for fine-grained 3D editing that does not require prior training. It utilizes geometric primitives to represent 3D shapes, allowing for precise structural modifications while maintaining the object's overall identity. By leveraging a pretrained vision-language model, the framework enables users to specify changes at the primitive level, which are then applied to the 3D model. The results show that Prox-E outperforms existing methods in preserving shape quality and fidelity to user instructions.

zh.title: 无训练的细粒度3D编辑新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种无训练的框架Prox-E，用于细粒度的3D编辑。该框架利用几何原语和视觉-语言模型，能够在保持物体整体身份的同时，实现局部结构的变化。首先，它将输入的3D形状抽象为一组紧凑的几何原语，然后通过预训练的视觉-语言模型对这些原语进行编辑。实验表明，该方法在身份保持、形状质量和指令保真度方面优于现有的多种方法。

papers.title: Learning while Deploying: Fleet-Scale Reinforcement Learning for Generalist Robot Policies
papers.abstract: Abstract Learning While Deploying framework enables continuous improvement of Vision-Language-Action policies through fleet-scale offline-to-online reinforcement learning with distributed robot experience and human interventions.  AI-generated summary Generalist robot policies increasingly benefit from large-scale pretraining, but offline data alone is insufficient for robust real-world deployment. Deployed robots encounter distribution shifts, long-tail failures, task variations, and human correction opportunities that fixed demonstration datasets cannot fully capture. We present Learning While Deploying (LWD), a fleet-scale offline-to-online reinforcement learning framework for continual post-training of generalist Vision-Language-Action (VLA) policies. Starting from a pretrained VLA policy, LWD closes the loop between deployment, shared physical experience, policy improvement, and redeployment by using autonomous rollouts and human interventions collected across a robot fleet. To stabilize learning from heterogeneous, sparse-reward fleet data, LWD combines Distributional Implicit Value Learning (DIVL) for robust value estimation with Q-learning via Adjoint Matching (QAM) for policy extraction in flow-based VLA action generators. We validate LWD on a fleet of 16 dual-arm robots across eight real-world manipulation tasks, including semantic grocery restocking and 3--5 minute long-horizon tasks. A single generalist policy improves as fleet experience accumulates, reaching an average success rate of 95%, with the largest gains on long-horizon tasks.

ru.title: Обучение робота во время работы: непрерывное совершенствование через опыт парка машин
ru.desc: Статья представляет Learning While Deploying (LWD) — фреймворк для непрерывного улучшения Vision-Language-Action политик роботов через обучение с подкреплением в режиме онлайн на данных с парка роботов. После предварительного обучения универсальная политика улучшается на основе реального опыта развёрнутых роботов и человеческих вмешательств, которые собираются с целого флота. Для стабилизации обучения на разнородных данных с редкими вознаграждениями используются методы DIVL для робастной оценки значений и QAM для извлечения политики. На флоте из 16 двухручных манипуляторов подтверждена эффективность подхода — единая политика достигла среднего показателя успеха в 95% на различных манипуляционных задачах.

en.title: Continuous Learning for Robots: Improving Policies in Real-Time
en.desc: The Learning While Deploying (LWD) framework enhances Vision-Language-Action (VLA) policies by enabling continuous learning from real-world robot experiences. It addresses challenges like distribution shifts and task variations that arise during deployment, which traditional offline datasets cannot handle. By integrating autonomous rollouts and human feedback, LWD allows for ongoing policy refinement and improvement. The framework employs advanced techniques like Distributional Implicit Value Learning and Q-learning to ensure robust learning from diverse and sparse data, achieving high success rates in complex manipulation tasks.

zh.title: 持续改进机器人策略的学习框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为“在部署中学习”（Learning While Deploying, LWD）的框架，旨在通过离线到在线的强化学习，持续改进视觉-语言-动作（VLA）策略。该框架利用分布式机器人经验和人类干预，解决了机器人在实际部署中遇到的分布变化和任务变异等问题。LWD结合了分布式隐式价值学习（DIVL）和基于伴随匹配的Q学习（QAM），以稳定从异质稀疏奖励数据中学习。实验表明，LWD在16个双臂机器人上进行的八个真实世界操作任务中，成功率达到了95%。

papers.title: Let ViT Speak: Generative Language-Image Pre-training
papers.abstract: Abstract GenLIP is a minimalist generative pretraining framework for Vision Transformers that directly predicts language tokens from visual tokens using language modeling, offering simplicity, scalability, and competitive performance in multimodal tasks.  AI-generated summary In this paper, we present Generative Language-Image Pre-training (GenLIP), a minimalist generative pretraining framework for Vision Transformers (ViTs) designed for multimodal large language models (MLLMs). To better align vision encoders with the autoregressive nature of LLMs, GenLIP trains a ViT to predict language tokens directly from visual tokens using a standard language modeling objective, without contrastive batch construction or an additional text decoder. This design offers three key advantages: (1) Simplicity: a single transformer jointly models visual and textual tokens; (2) Scalability: it scales effectively with both data and model size; and (3) Performance: it achieves competitive or superior results across diverse multimodal benchmarks. Trained on 8B samples from Recap-DataComp-1B, GenLIP matches or surpasses strong baselines despite using substantially less pretraining data. After continued pretraining on multi-resolution images at native aspect ratios, GenLIP further improves on detail-sensitive tasks such as OCR and chart understanding, making it a strong foundation for vision encoders in MLLMs.

ru.title: Простота и масштабируемость мультимодального обучения через прямое предсказание языковых токенов
ru.desc: GenLIP — это минималистичный фреймворк для предварительного обучения Vision Transformers, который напрямую предсказывает токены языка из визуальных токенов, используя стандартный objective языкового моделирования. Модель не требует контрастивной конструкции батчей или отдельного текстового декодера, что обеспечивает простоту архитектуры и лучшую интеграцию с большими языковыми моделями. Фреймворк демонстрирует отличную масштабируемость по данным и параметрам модели, достигая конкурентных результатов на мультимодальных бенчмарках при использовании существенно меньшего объема предварительного обучения. Дополнительное обучение на мультирезолюционных изображениях улучшает производительность на задачах, требующих высокой детализации, таких как распознавание текста и анализ диаграмм.

en.title: GenLIP: Simplifying Vision and Language Integration
en.desc: GenLIP is a new framework that simplifies how Vision Transformers (ViTs) learn to connect images and text. It directly predicts language tokens from visual tokens using a language modeling approach, eliminating the need for complex batch constructions or separate text decoders. This method not only makes the model easier to implement but also allows it to scale well with more data and larger models. As a result, GenLIP achieves strong performance on various multimodal tasks, even outperforming other models while using less training data.

zh.title: 简约高效的视觉-语言预训练框架
zh.desc: GenLIP是一种简约的生成预训练框架，专为视觉变换器（ViTs）设计，能够直接从视觉标记预测语言标记。该框架通过标准的语言建模目标训练ViT，避免了对比批次构建或额外文本解码器的需求。GenLIP的设计具有三个主要优点：简单性、可扩展性和性能。经过在8B样本上的训练，GenLIP在多模态基准测试中表现出色，尤其在细节敏感的任务上进一步提升了效果。

papers.title: Stable-GFlowNet: Toward Diverse and Robust LLM Red-Teaming via Contrastive Trajectory Balance
papers.abstract: Abstract Stable-GFN addresses training instability and mode collapse in generative flow networks for large language model red-teaming through partition function elimination and robust masking techniques.  AI-generated summary Large Language Model (LLM) Red-Teaming, which proactively identifies vulnerabilities of LLMs, is an essential process for ensuring safety. Finding effective and diverse attacks in red-teaming is important, but achieving both is challenging. Generative Flow Networks (GFNs) that perform distribution matching are a promising methods, but they are notorious for training instability and mode collapse. In particular, unstable rewards in red-teaming accelerate mode collapse. We propose Stable-GFN (S-GFN), which eliminates partition function Z estimation in GFN and reduces training instability. S-GFN avoids Z-estimation through pairwise comparisons and employs a robust masking methodology against noisy rewards. Additionally, we propose a fluency stabilizer to prevent the model from getting stuck in local optima that produce gibberish. S-GFN provides more stable training while maintaining the optimal policy of GFN. We demonstrate the overwhelming attack performance and diversity of S-GFN across various settings.

ru.title: Стабильное обучение потоковых сетей для безопасного красного тестирования больших моделей
ru.desc: Статья представляет Stable-GFN, метод для улучшения тренировки генеративных потоковых сетей при красном тестировании больших языковых моделей. Основная проблема заключается в нестабильности обучения и коллапсе режимов, которые усугубляются нестабильными наградами в процессе поиска уязвимостей. Авторы предлагают исключить оценку функции разбиения и использовать попарные сравнения вместе с устойчивым маскированием для снижения нестабильности. Дополнительно они добавляют стабилизатор беглости, чтобы модель не застревала в локальных оптимумах, генерирующих бессмыслицу.

en.title: Stable Training for Effective Red-Teaming in LLMs
en.desc: The paper introduces Stable-GFN (S-GFN), a method designed to improve the training stability of Generative Flow Networks (GFNs) used in red-teaming large language models (LLMs). It addresses issues like mode collapse and unstable rewards by eliminating the need for partition function estimation, which is often a source of instability. S-GFN employs pairwise comparisons and a robust masking technique to handle noisy rewards effectively. The method also includes a fluency stabilizer to ensure that the generated outputs remain coherent and diverse, leading to enhanced performance in identifying vulnerabilities in LLMs.

zh.title: 稳定生成流网络，提升红队攻击效果
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种名为Stable-GFN（S-GFN）的生成流网络模型，旨在解决大语言模型红队训练中的不稳定性和模式崩溃问题。通过消除分区函数的估计，S-GFN能够减少训练过程中的不稳定性，并采用稳健的掩蔽技术来应对噪声奖励。该模型还引入了流畅性稳定器，以防止模型陷入产生无意义输出的局部最优解。实验结果表明，S-GFN在多种设置下展现了卓越的攻击性能和多样性。

papers.title: From Skill Text to Skill Structure: The Scheduling-Structural-Logical Representation for Agent Skills
papers.abstract: Abstract Structured representation of agent skills disentangles scheduling, execution, and logic components, improving performance in skill discovery and risk assessment tasks.  AI-generated summary LLM agents increasingly rely on reusable skills, capability packages that combine instructions, control flow, constraints, and tool calls. In most current agent systems, however, skills are still represented by text-heavy artifacts, including SKILL.md-style documents and structured records whose machine-usable evidence remains embedded largely in natural-language descriptions. This poses a challenge for skill-centered agent systems: managing skill collections and using skills to support agent both require reasoning over invocation interfaces, execution structure, and concrete side effects that are often entangled in a single textual surface. An explicit representation of skill knowledge may therefore help make these artifacts easier for machines to acquire and leverage. Drawing on Memory Organization Packets, Script Theory, and Conceptual Dependency from Schank and Abelson's classical work on linguistic knowledge representation, we introduce what is, to our knowledge, the first structured representation for agent skill artifacts that disentangles skill-level scheduling signals, scene-level execution structure, and logic-level action and resource-use evidence: the Scheduling-Structural-Logical (SSL) representation. We instantiate SSL with an LLM-based normalizer and evaluate it on a corpus of skills in two tasks, Skill Discovery and Risk Assessment, and superiorly outperform the text-only baselines: in Skill Discovery, SSL improves MRR from 0.573 to 0.707; in Risk Assessment, it improves macro F1 from 0.744 to 0.787. These findings reveal that explicit, source-grounded structure makes agent skills easier to search and review. They also suggest that SSL is best understood as a practical step toward more inspectable, reusable, and operationally actionable skill representations for agent systems, rather than as a finished standard or an end-to-end mechanism for managing and using skills.
affiliations: Department of Chinese Language and Literature, Peking University
Key Laboratory of Computational Linguistics, Ministry of Education, Peking University
School of Computer Science, Peking University

ru.title: Структурированное представление навыков для более интеллектуальных агентов
ru.desc: Статья предлагает структурированное представление навыков агентов, называемое SSL (Scheduling-Structural-Logical), которое разделяет информацию о планировании, выполнении и логике действий. В существующих системах навыки обычно описываются текстовыми документами, где важная информация для машин остаётся скрыта в естественном языке. Авторы используют классические работы по теории языковых знаний и разработали нормализатор на основе LLM для преобразования текстовых описаний в структурированный формат. Экспериментальная оценка показала, что SSL значительно улучшает поиск навыков (MRR с 0.573 до 0.707) и оценку рисков (F1 с 0.744 до 0.787) по сравнению с текстовыми базовыми решениями.

en.title: Unlocking Agent Skills with Structured Representation
en.desc: This paper introduces a new way to represent agent skills called the Scheduling-Structural-Logical (SSL) representation. It separates the different components of skills, such as scheduling, execution, and logic, making it easier for machines to understand and use these skills. By using SSL, the authors show that agents can perform better in tasks like Skill Discovery and Risk Assessment compared to traditional text-heavy methods. The results indicate that a structured approach to skill representation enhances the efficiency and effectiveness of agent systems.

zh.title: 结构化技能表示，提升代理性能
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的结构化表示方法，称为调度-结构-逻辑（SSL）表示，用于代理技能的表示。该方法将技能的调度信号、执行结构和逻辑证据分开，从而提高了技能发现和风险评估任务的性能。通过使用基于大语言模型的归一化器，SSL在技能发现和风险评估任务中显著优于传统的文本表示方法。研究结果表明，明确的结构化表示使得代理技能更易于搜索和审查，推动了可重用和可操作的技能表示的发展。

papers.title: Trees to Flows and Back: Unifying Decision Trees and Diffusion Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Decision trees and diffusion models are mathematically unified through a shared optimization principle called Global Trajectory Score Matching, enabling efficient generative models and neural network distillation methods.  AI-generated summary Decision trees and diffusion models are ostensibly disparate model classes, one discrete and hierarchical, the other continuous and dynamic. This work unifies the two by establishing a crisp mathematical correspondence between hierarchical decision trees and diffusion processes in appropriate limiting regimes. Our unification reveals a shared optimization principle: Global Trajectory Score Matching (GTSM), for which gradient boosting (in an idealized version) is asymptotically optimal. We underscore the conceptual value of our work through two key practical instantiations: \treeflow, which achieves competitive generation quality on tabular data with higher fidelity and a 2\times computational speedup, and \dsmtree, a novel distillation method that transfers hierarchical decision logic into neural networks, matching teacher performance within 2\% on many benchmarks.
affiliations: School of Computation, Information and Technology, Technical University of Munich, Germany

ru.title: Деревья решений и диффузия: единая математическая основа
ru.desc: В работе установлена математическая связь между деревьями решений и диффузионными моделями через общий принцип оптимизации, называемый Global Trajectory Score Matching. Авторы показывают, что градиентный бустинг асимптотически оптимален для этого принципа. На основе этого объединения предложены два практических метода: TreeFlow для генерации табличных данных с ускорением в 2 раза и DSMTree для дистилляции логики деревьев в нейронные сети. Эти подходы демонстрируют эффективность предложенного теоретического фреймворка.

en.title: Unifying Decision Trees and Diffusion Models for Enhanced AI
en.desc: This paper presents a unifying framework for decision trees and diffusion models using a mathematical principle called Global Trajectory Score Matching (GTSM). It shows that despite their differences, both model types can be related through this optimization approach, leading to improved generative modeling and neural network distillation. The authors introduce two practical applications: 	reeflow, which enhances the generation of tabular data with better quality and faster computation, and 	ext{dsmtree}, a method that effectively transfers decision-making logic from trees to neural networks. This work highlights the potential for cross-pollination between different machine learning paradigms, improving efficiency and performance.

zh.title: 决策树与扩散模型的数学统一
zh.desc: 本文通过一个共享的优化原则——全局轨迹得分匹配（GTSM），将决策树和扩散模型在数学上统一起来。这两种模型表面上看似不同，一个是离散的层次结构，另一个是连续的动态过程。我们的研究揭示了层次决策树与扩散过程之间的数学对应关系，并指出在理想化条件下，梯度提升是渐近最优的。我们还通过两个实际应用展示了这一统一的概念价值，分别是	reeflow和	ext{dsmtree}，前者在表格数据生成上实现了更高的保真度和2倍的计算速度提升，后者则将层次决策逻辑转移到神经网络中，多个基准测试中与教师模型的表现相差不超过2%。

papers.title: When Do Diffusion Models learn to Generate Multiple Objects?
papers.abstract: Abstract Diffusion models struggle with multi-object generation due to scene complexity rather than concept imbalance, with counting being particularly challenging in low-data regimes.  AI-generated summary Text-to-image diffusion models achieve impressive visual fidelity, yet they remain unreliable in multi-object generation. Despite extensive empirical evidence of these failures, the underlying causes remain unclear. We begin by asking how much of this limitation arises from the data itself. To disentangle data effects, we consider two regimes across different dataset sizes: (1) concept generalization, where each individual concept is observed during training under potentially imbalanced data distributions, and (2) compositional generalization, where specific combinations of concepts are systematically held out. To study these regimes, we introduce mosaic (Multi-Object Spatial relations, AttrIbution, Counting), a controlled framework for dataset generation. By training diffusion models on mosaic, we find that scene complexity plays a dominant role rather than concept imbalance, and that counting is uniquely difficult to learn in low-data regimes. Moreover, compositional generalization collapses as more concept combinations are held out during training. These findings highlight fundamental limitations of diffusion models and motivate stronger inductive biases and data design for robust multi-object compositional generation.
affiliations: Visual Geometry Group, University of Oxford

ru.title: Сложность сцены, а не дисбаланс данных, — ключевой вызов для многообъектной генерации
ru.desc: В работе исследуется проблема генерации множественных объектов в диффузионных моделях текст-в-изображение. Авторы показывают, что основной причиной ошибок является сложность сцены, а не дисбаланс в данных обучения. Они вводят контролируемый фреймворк MOSAIC для систематического изучения двух режимов: обобщения отдельных концепций и композиционального обобщения комбинаций концепций. Результаты демонстрируют, что подсчёт объектов особенно сложен при ограниченном объёме данных, а композиональное обобщение деградирует при исключении комбинаций концепций из обучения.

en.title: Unlocking Multi-Object Generation in Diffusion Models
en.desc: This paper investigates the challenges faced by diffusion models in generating images with multiple objects, particularly focusing on scene complexity rather than just data imbalance. The authors introduce a new framework called mosaic, which helps analyze how different dataset sizes and combinations of concepts affect model performance. Their findings reveal that counting objects is especially difficult when there is limited training data, and that the ability to generalize across different combinations of concepts deteriorates when more combinations are excluded from training. The study emphasizes the need for improved data design and stronger inductive biases to enhance the robustness of multi-object generation in diffusion models.

zh.title: 揭示扩散模型在多对象生成中的局限性
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了扩散模型在生成多对象时的挑战，特别是在数据量较少的情况下。研究发现，场景复杂性是主要问题，而不是概念不平衡。通过引入马赛克框架，作者分析了不同数据集大小对模型性能的影响。结果表明，计数在低数据环境中尤其难以学习，且随着训练中排除更多概念组合，模型的组合泛化能力显著下降。

papers.title: End-to-End Autoregressive Image Generation with 1D Semantic Tokenizer
papers.abstract: Abstract End-to-end training of autoregressive image models with joint reconstruction and generation optimization achieves state-of-the-art results on ImageNet 256x256 generation.  AI-generated summary Autoregressive image modeling relies on visual tokenizers to compress images into compact latent representations. We design an end-to-end training pipeline that jointly optimizes reconstruction and generation, enabling direct supervision from generation results to the tokenizer. This contrasts with prior two-stage approaches that train tokenizers and generative models separately. We further investigate leveraging vision foundation models to improve 1D tokenizers for autoregressive modeling. Our autoregressive generative model achieves strong empirical results, including a state-of-the-art FID score of 1.48 without guidance on ImageNet 256x256 generation.

ru.title: Совместная оптимизация токенизатора и генератора для авторегрессионной генерации изображений
ru.desc: В работе предлагается сквозное обучение авторегрессионных моделей для генерации изображений путём совместной оптимизации реконструкции и генерации. Визуальные токенизаторы сжимают изображения в компактные скрытые представления, которые затем используются в авторегрессионной модели. Ключевое отличие в том, что токенизатор и генеративная модель обучаются совместно, получая прямую обратную связь от результатов генерации, в отличие от традиционного двухэтапного подхода. Модель достигает впечатляющих результатов, включая лучший FIDScore 1.48 при генерации изображений на датасете ImageNet 256x256.

en.title: Revolutionizing Image Generation with Joint Optimization
en.desc: This paper presents a novel approach to training autoregressive image models by integrating reconstruction and generation optimization in a single end-to-end pipeline. By using visual tokenizers, the model compresses images into compact representations, allowing for more efficient image generation. Unlike previous methods that trained tokenizers and generative models separately, this approach enables direct feedback from generation results to improve the tokenizer. The authors also explore the use of vision foundation models to enhance the performance of 1D tokenizers, achieving a state-of-the-art FID score of 1.48 on ImageNet 256x256 generation.

zh.title: 自回归图像模型的端到端训练新突破
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种端到端训练的自回归图像模型，通过联合重建和生成优化，在ImageNet 256x256生成任务中取得了最先进的结果。自回归图像建模依赖于视觉标记器，将图像压缩为紧凑的潜在表示。我们设计的训练流程能够直接从生成结果对标记器进行监督，这与之前分阶段训练标记器和生成模型的方法不同。我们的自回归生成模型在ImageNet 256x256生成任务中实现了1.48的最先进FID分数，显示出强大的实证结果。

papers.title: Online Self-Calibration Against Hallucination in Vision-Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Online self-calibration framework using Monte Carlo tree search and dual-granularity reward mechanism improves vision-language model accuracy by addressing hallucination through preference data construction and direct preference optimization.  AI-generated summary Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) often suffer from hallucinations, generating descriptions that include visual details absent from the input image. Recent preference alignment methods typically rely on supervision distilled from stronger models such as GPT. However, this offline paradigm introduces a Supervision-Perception Mismatch: the student model is forced to align with fine-grained details beyond its perceptual capacity, learning to guess rather than to see. To obtain reliable self-supervision for online learning, we identify a Generative-Discriminative Gap within LVLMs, where models exhibit higher accuracy on discriminative verification than open-ended generation. Leveraging this capability, we propose Online Self-CAlibRation (OSCAR), a framework that integrates Monte Carlo Tree Search with a Dual-Granularity Reward Mechanism to construct preference data and iteratively refines the model via Direct Preference Optimization. Extensive experiments demonstrate that OSCAR achieves state-of-the-art performance on hallucination benchmarks while improving general multimodal capabilities.
affiliations: Institute of Information Engineering, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing, China
School of Cyber Security, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing, China

ru.title: Самокалибровка видео-языковых моделей через онлайн-обучение с предпочтениями
ru.desc: В статье предложен фреймворк OSCAR для устранения галлюцинаций в больших визуально-языковых моделях через онлайн-обучение с самокалибровкой. Авторы выявили проблему несоответствия между восприятием и надзором: студент-модель вынуждена выравниваться с деталями, которые она не может правильно воспринять. Они используют генеративно-дискриминативный разрыв в LVLMs, применяя поиск по дереву Монте-Карло и двухуровневый механизм награды для построения данных предпочтений. Модель итеративно улучшается через оптимизацию прямых предпочтений, достигая лучших результатов на бенчмарках галлюцинаций.

en.title: Enhancing LVLM Accuracy with OSCAR: A New Approach to Combat Hallucinations
en.desc: This paper introduces a new framework called Online Self-CAlibRation (OSCAR) to enhance the accuracy of Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) by addressing the issue of hallucinations. Hallucinations occur when models generate incorrect visual details that are not present in the input images. OSCAR utilizes Monte Carlo Tree Search and a Dual-Granularity Reward Mechanism to create preference data that helps the model learn more effectively. By focusing on direct preference optimization, OSCAR improves the model's ability to generate accurate descriptions while maintaining strong multimodal performance.

zh.title: 在线自校准，提升视觉-语言模型的准确性
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种在线自校准框架，名为OSCAR，结合了蒙特卡洛树搜索和双粒度奖励机制，以提高视觉-语言模型的准确性。该方法通过构建偏好数据和直接优化偏好，解决了模型在生成描述时出现的幻觉问题。研究表明，传统的偏好对齐方法存在监督与感知不匹配的问题，导致模型学习到的内容超出了其感知能力。通过识别生成-判别差距，OSCAR能够在自监督学习中实现更可靠的性能，显著提升多模态能力。

papers.title: Learning to Act and Cooperate for Distributed Black-Box Consensus Optimization
papers.abstract: Abstract A trajectory-driven framework uses large language models to guide agent behavior and cooperation patterns in distributed black-box consensus optimization, improving solution quality and efficiency.  AI-generated summary Distributed blackbox consensus optimization is a fundamental problem in multi-agent systems, where agents must improve a global objective using only local objective queries and limited neighbor communication. Existing methods largely rely on handcrafted update rules and static cooperation patterns, which often struggle to balance local adaptation, global coordination, and communication efficiency in heterogeneous nonconvex environments. In this paper, we take an initial step toward trajectory-driven self-design for distributed black-box consensus optimization. We first redesign the agent-level swarm dynamics with an adaptive internal mechanism tailored to decentralized consensus settings, improving the balance between exploration, convergence, and local escape. Built on top of this adaptive execution layer, we propose Learning to Act and Cooperate (LACMAS), a trajectorydriven framework in which large language models provide sparse highlevel guidance for shaping both agentinternal action behaviors and agentexternal cooperation patterns from historical optimization trajectories. We further introduce a phased cognitive scheduling strategy to activate different forms of adaptation in a resource-aware manner. Experiments on standard distributed black-box benchmarks and real-world distributed tasks show that LAC-MAS consistently improves solution quality, convergence efficiency, and communication efficiency over strong baselines, suggesting a practical route from handcrafted distributed coordination toward self-designing multi-agent optimization systems.
affiliations: School of Computer Science and Engineering, South China University of Technology, Guangzhou, China

ru.title: От ручной разработки к самопроектированию: LLM-управляемая оптимизация в многоагентных системах
ru.desc: В работе предложена система LACMAS, которая использует большие языковые модели для автоматического проектирования поведения агентов в распределённой оптимизации чёрного ящика. Фреймворк анализирует исторические траектории оптимизации и генерирует разреженные высокоуровневые рекомендации для адаптации как внутренних механизмов отдельных агентов, так и паттернов их кооперации. Введена когнитивная стратегия фазового планирования, которая активирует различные формы адаптации с учётом доступных ресурсов. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют улучшение качества решений, скорости сходимости и эффективности коммуникации по сравнению с существующими методами.

en.title: Empowering Agents with Language for Smarter Collaboration
en.desc: This paper presents a new approach to improve how multiple agents work together to solve optimization problems without direct communication. It introduces a framework called Learning to Act and Cooperate (LACMAS), which uses large language models to guide agent behaviors and cooperation based on past experiences. The framework enhances the agents' ability to adapt their actions and collaborate effectively, balancing exploration and convergence in complex environments. Experimental results demonstrate that LACMAS outperforms traditional methods, leading to better solutions and more efficient communication among agents.

zh.title: 智能体自设计的优化新路径
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种基于轨迹驱动的框架，利用大型语言模型来指导多智能体系统中的行为和合作模式，从而提高分布式黑箱共识优化的解决方案质量和效率。现有方法主要依赖手工设计的更新规则和静态合作模式，难以在异构非凸环境中平衡局部适应、全局协调和通信效率。我们重新设计了智能体级别的群体动态，采用适应性内部机制，以改善探索、收敛和局部逃逸之间的平衡。通过引入学习行动与合作（LACMAS）框架，我们展示了如何利用历史优化轨迹为智能体行为和合作模式提供高层次的稀疏指导。

papers.title: Themis: Training Robust Multilingual Code Reward Models for Flexible Multi-Criteria Scoring
papers.abstract: Abstract Researchers introduce Themis-RM, a suite of multilingual code reward models trained on a large preference dataset to enable flexible multi-criteria scoring for code generation tasks.  AI-generated summary Reward models (RMs) have become an indispensable fixture of the language model (LM) post-training playbook, enabling policy alignment and test-time scaling. Research on the application of RMs in code generation, however, has been comparatively sparse, with existing work largely focusing on execution feedback. This choice constrains post-training to optimizing functional correctness over self-contained executable code. In this work, we examine the training and evaluation of multilingual, multi-criteria code RMs. To this end, we first compile Themis-CodeRewardBench, a benchmark to evaluate code RMs across five preference dimensions (i.e., criteria) and eight programming languages, on which we profile 50+ code, math, and general-purpose RMs. Observing the limited proficiency of current RMs beyond scoring for functional correctness, we develop Themis-CodePreference, the largest open-source collection of code preferences to date (more than 350k preference pairs), and use it to train Themis-RM, a suite of multilingual code reward models for flexible multi-criteria scoring, ranging in size from 600M to 32B parameters. Our experiments and ablations demonstrate positive scaling trends, strong cross-lingual transfer when training on diverse preferences, and the importance of multi-criteria training for reliable code reward modeling.
affiliations: Center for Artificial Intelligence and Data Science, University of Würzburg
National Research Center for Applied Cybersecurity ATHENE

ru.title: Гибкое многокритериальное оценивание сгенерированного кода с помощью многоязычных моделей вознаграждения
ru.desc: Исследователи представляют Themis-RM, набор многоязычных моделей вознаграждения для кода, обученных на большом датасете предпочтений. Авторы создали Themis-CodeRewardBench для оценки моделей вознаграждения по пяти критериям качества на восьми языках программирования. Они собрали Themis-CodePreference — крупнейший открытый набор предпочтений кода с более чем 350k пар примеров для обучения. Эксперименты показывают положительные тренды масштабирования, хорошую кросс-лингвальную передачу знаний и важность многокритериального обучения для надёжного оценивания качества сгенерированного кода.

en.title: Empowering Code Generation with Multilingual Reward Models
en.desc: Themis-RM is a new set of multilingual code reward models designed to improve code generation by using a large dataset of preferences. These models allow for flexible scoring based on multiple criteria, rather than just focusing on whether the code runs correctly. The researchers created a benchmark called Themis-CodeRewardBench to evaluate these models across different programming languages and criteria. Their findings show that training on diverse preferences enhances the models' performance and reliability in assessing code quality.

zh.title: 多标准评分的代码奖励模型
zh.desc: 本文介绍了Themis-RM，这是一个多语言代码奖励模型的套件，旨在支持灵活的多标准评分，用于代码生成任务。研究表明，现有的奖励模型主要集中在功能正确性上，缺乏对多种标准的评估。为此，作者创建了Themis-CodeRewardBench基准，评估五个偏好维度和八种编程语言的代码奖励模型。通过开发Themis-CodePreference，作者收集了超过35万个代码偏好对，并利用这些数据训练了Themis-RM，以实现更可靠的代码奖励建模。

papers.title: LASE: Language-Adversarial Speaker Encoding for Indic Cross-Script Identity Preservation
papers.abstract: Abstract A language-adversarial speaker encoder (LASE) is proposed to address cross-script voice cloning issues by training with contrastive loss and gradient-reversal learning to produce language-uninformative yet speaker-informative embeddings.  AI-generated summary A speaker encoder used in multilingual voice cloning should treat the same speaker identically regardless of which script the audio was uttered in. Off-the-shelf encoders do not, and the failure is accent-conditional. On a 1043-pair Western-accented voice corpus across English, Hindi, Telugu, and Tamil, WavLM-base-plus-sv loses 0.082 absolute cosine similarity when the same voice changes script and ECAPA-TDNN loses 0.105. On a 1369-pair Indian-accented voice corpus, the gap shrinks to 0.006 (WavLM-SV) and 0.044 (ECAPA-TDNN). The leak is largest where it matters most for cross-script TTS: when a system projects a non-Indic-trained voice into Indic scripts. We present LASE (Language-Adversarial Speaker Encoder), a small projection head over frozen WavLM-base-plus trained with two losses: a supervised contrastive loss over voice identity, and a gradient-reversal cross-entropy against a 4-language classifier that pushes the embedding to be language-uninformative while remaining speaker-informative. Trained on 1118 quality-gated cross-script pairs synthesised from 8 commercial multilingual voices, LASE's residual gap is consistent with zero on both corpora (Delta = 0.013 Western, Delta = 0.026 Indian; both bootstrap 95% CIs include zero) and amplifies the cross-script-vs-floor margin 2.4-2.7x over both baselines. An ECAPA+GRL ablation shows the GRL objective improves either backbone but the WavLM choice contributes too. In synthetic multi-speaker diarisation, LASE matches ECAPA-TDNN on cross-script speaker recall (0.788 vs 0.789) with ~100x less training data. We release the r1 checkpoint, both corpora, and the bootstrap recipe.

ru.title: Универсальный кодировщик голоса, независимый от языка и скрипта
ru.desc: В работе предлагается Language-Adversarial Speaker Encoder (LASE) — кодировщик голоса для решения проблемы кроссязыковой клонирования голоса. Модель обучается с помощью контрастивной потерь и обращения градиента (gradient reversal), что позволяет получать эмбеддинги, содержащие информацию о говорящем, но не содержащие информацию о языке. Экспериименты на корпусах с английским, хинди, телугу и тамильским языками показали, что LASE значительно уменьшает разницу в косинусном сходстве для одного и того же голоса на разных языках. Метод демонстрирует эффективность даже при обучении на синтетических данных и требует значительно меньше обучающих примеров, чем существующие подходы.

en.title: Unifying Voice Cloning Across Languages with LASE
en.desc: The paper introduces a novel approach called the Language-Adversarial Speaker Encoder (LASE) to improve voice cloning across different scripts. LASE utilizes a combination of supervised contrastive loss and gradient-reversal learning to create embeddings that are informative about the speaker but not about the language. This method addresses the issue where traditional encoders fail to recognize the same speaker consistently when the script changes, particularly in multilingual contexts. The results show that LASE significantly reduces the discrepancy in speaker recognition across scripts, achieving near-zero residual gaps in performance on tested voice corpora.

zh.title: 跨脚本语音克隆的新突破：LASE
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种语言对抗说话人编码器（LASE），旨在解决跨脚本语音克隆问题。通过对比损失和梯度反转学习，LASE能够生成不受语言影响但保留说话人特征的嵌入。实验表明，LASE在多语言语音合成中表现优异，能够有效减少不同脚本之间的相似度差距。最终，LASE在合成多说话人分离任务中，使用的训练数据量比传统方法少了约100倍，仍能达到相似的效果。

papers.title: MASCing: Configurable Mixture-of-Experts Behavior via Activation Steering Masks
papers.abstract: Abstract MASCing is a framework that enables flexible reconfiguration of Mixture-of-Experts model behavior for safety objectives through steering matrices and routing gate modifications without requiring retraining.  AI-generated summary Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures in Large Language Models (LLMs) have significantly reduced inference costs through sparse activation. However, this sparse activation paradigm also introduces new safety challenges. Since only a subset of experts is engaged for each input, model behavior becomes coupled to routing decisions, yielding a difficult-to-control mechanism that can vary across safety-relevant scenarios. At the same time, adapting model behavior through full fine-tuning or retraining is costly, especially when developers need to rapidly configure the same model for different safety objectives. We present MASCing (MoE Activation Steering Configuration), the first framework that enables flexible reconfiguration of MoE behavior across diverse safety scenarios without retraining. MASCing uses an LSTM-based surrogate model to capture cross-layer routing dependencies and map routing logits to downstream behaviors. It then optimizes a steering matrix to identify behavior-relevant expert circuits and, at inference time, applies steering masks to the routing gates to override expert selection. This enables targeted enhancement or suppression of specific behaviors while preserving general language utility. To demonstrate its reconfigurability, we apply MASCing to two different safety-related objectives and observe consistent gains with negligible overhead across seven open-source MoE models. For multi-turn jailbreak defense, it improves the average defense success rate from 52.5% to 83.9%, with gains of up to 89.2%. For adult-content generation, MASCing enables models to comply with such requests that would otherwise be refused, increasing the average generation success rate from 52.6% to 82.0%, with gains of up to 93.0%. These results establish MASCing as a practical, lightweight, and flexible framework for scenario-specific safety reconfiguration in MoE models.

ru.title: Управление экспертами MoE без переобучения: гибкая конфигурация безопасности
ru.desc: MASCing — это фреймворк для гибкой переконфигурации поведения моделей Mixture-of-Experts с целью повышения безопасности через матрицы управления и модификацию gate'ов маршрутизации без переобучения. Авторы используют LSTM-модель для захвата кроссслойных зависимостей маршрутизации и оптимизируют матрицу управления, чтобы выявить цепочки экспертов, релевантные для целевого поведения. На этапе инференса система применяет маски к gate'ам маршрутизации, чтобы избирательно подавлять или усиливать определённые поведения модели. Фреймворк продемонстрировал значительное улучшение защиты от jailbreak-атак (с 52,5% до 83,9%) и управления генерацией контента с минимальными вычислительными затратами.

en.title: Flexible Safety Reconfiguration for MoE Models with MASCing
en.desc: MASCing is a novel framework designed to enhance the safety of Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models without the need for retraining. It allows for the flexible adjustment of model behavior by modifying routing gates and applying steering matrices, which helps in managing safety-related scenarios effectively. By utilizing an LSTM-based surrogate model, MASCing captures the dependencies in routing decisions and optimizes expert selection for specific tasks. This approach not only improves safety outcomes significantly but also maintains the overall performance of the language model.

zh.title: 灵活安全配置的混合专家模型
zh.desc: MASCing是一个框架，能够灵活地重新配置混合专家模型的行为，以满足安全目标，而无需重新训练。该框架通过使用基于LSTM的替代模型来捕捉跨层路由依赖关系，并将路由逻辑映射到下游行为。MASCing优化一个引导矩阵，以识别与行为相关的专家电路，并在推理时应用引导掩码来覆盖专家选择。通过这种方式，MASCing可以在保持语言通用性的同时，有针对性地增强或抑制特定行为。

papers.title: Better Models, Faster Training: Sigmoid Attention for single-cell Foundation Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Sigmoid attention improves biological foundation model training by providing better representations, faster convergence, and enhanced stability compared to softmax attention through bounded derivatives and diagonal Jacobian structure.  AI-generated summary Training stable biological foundation models requires rethinking attention mechanisms: we find that using sigmoid attention as a drop in replacement for softmax attention a) produces better learned representations: on six diverse single-cell datasets, sigmoid achieves 25% higher cell-type separation, better cell-type cohesion metrics, and lower validation loss, b) faster training, models with sigmoid attention train up to 10% faster than their softmax counterparts, and c) more stable training by eliminating inherent sources of instability in softmax attention. We establish that sigmoid attention has globally bounded derivatives (leq 0.25) as opposed to softmax, and a diagonal Jacobian structure in contrast with softmax's dense coupling, which together help alleviate training instabilities. In stress tests on 160M-parameter bidirectional attention models trained without gradient clipping on 8K-token sequences, softmax diverges catastrophically, with gradients exploding by four orders of magnitude, while sigmoid remains stable. Finally, we implement and open-source TritonSigmoid, an efficient GPU kernel that achieves 515 TFLOPS on H100 GPUs, outperforming both FlashAttention-2 and FlashSigmoid, with native padding support, which is essential for biological sequences. Our results establish sigmoid attention as both theoretically grounded and empirically superior for biological foundation models. Code is available at https://github.com/MSDLLCpapers/triton-sigmoid
affiliations: Merck & Co., Inc., Cambridge, MA, USA

ru.title: Сигмоидное внимание: путь к стабильному и эффективному обучению биологических моделей
ru.desc: В статье предлагается использовать сигмоидную функцию активации вместо софтмакса в механизме внимания для обучения биологических фундаментальных моделей. Авторы показывают, что сигмоидное внимание обеспечивает лучшие представления данных с 25% более высокой разделимостью типов клеток, ускоряет обучение на 10% и повышает его стабильность благодаря ограниченным производным и диагональной структуре матрицы Якобиана. В стресс-тестах на больших моделях софтмакс приводит к взрыву градиентов, тогда как сигмоидное внимание остаётся стабильным. Авторы реализуют эффективное GPU ядро TritonSigmoid, которое превосходит существующие оптимизированные реализации внимания на современных ускорителях.

en.title: Sigmoid Attention: A Stable and Efficient Alternative for Biological Models
en.desc: This paper introduces sigmoid attention as a more effective alternative to softmax attention for training biological foundation models. Sigmoid attention provides better representations, achieving 25% higher cell-type separation and improved stability during training. It also accelerates the training process, with models using sigmoid attention training up to 10% faster than those using softmax. The authors demonstrate that sigmoid attention has bounded derivatives and a diagonal Jacobian structure, which help prevent the training instabilities commonly associated with softmax attention.

zh.title: Sigmoid注意力：生物模型训练的新突破
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了Sigmoid注意力机制，旨在改善生物基础模型的训练效果。与传统的softmax注意力相比，Sigmoid注意力在表示能力、收敛速度和训练稳定性方面表现更佳。研究表明，Sigmoid注意力在六个不同的单细胞数据集上实现了25%的细胞类型分离度提升，并且训练速度提高了10%。此外，Sigmoid注意力的全局有界导数和对角雅可比结构有效消除了softmax注意力的训练不稳定性。

papers.title: Talker-T2AV: Joint Talking Audio-Video Generation with Autoregressive Diffusion Modeling
papers.abstract: Abstract Talker-T2AV presents an autoregressive diffusion framework for talking head synthesis that separates high-level cross-modal reasoning from low-level modality-specific refinement, improving lip-sync accuracy and cross-modal consistency.  AI-generated summary Joint audio-video generation models have shown that unified generation yields stronger cross-modal coherence than cascaded approaches. However, existing models couple modalities throughout denoising via pervasive attention, treating high-level semantics and low-level details in a fully entangled manner. This is suboptimal for talking head synthesis: while audio and facial motion are semantically correlated, their low-level realizations (acoustic signals and visual textures) follow distinct rendering processes. Enforcing joint modeling across all levels causes unnecessary entanglement and reduces efficiency. We propose Talker-T2AV, an autoregressive diffusion framework where high-level cross-modal modeling occurs in a shared backbone, while low-level refinement uses modality-specific decoders. A shared autoregressive language model jointly reasons over audio and video in a unified patch-level token space. Two lightweight diffusion transformer heads decode the hidden states into frame-level audio and video latents. Experiments on talking portrait benchmarks show Talker-T2AV outperforms dual-branch baselines in lip-sync accuracy, video quality, and audio quality, achieving stronger cross-modal consistency than cascaded pipelines.
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Раздельная обработка смысла и деталей для синтеза говорящей головы
ru.desc: Talker-T2AV представляет новый подход к синтезу говорящего лица, использующий диффузионную модель с разделением задач между высокоуровневым кросс-модальным рассуждением и низкоуровневой модально-специфичной обработкой. Авторы показывают, что совместная генерация аудио и видео эффективнее каскадных методов, но существующие модели создают избыточное переплетение модальностей через механизм внимания на всех уровнях. Предложенная архитектура использует общий автарегрессивный языковой трансформер для семантического выравнивания аудио и видео, а специализированные диффузионные декодеры независимо синтезируют аудиосигнал и видеоконтент. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют превосходство метода по синхронизации губ, качеству видео и аудио в сравнении с существующими подходами.

en.title: Enhancing Talking Head Synthesis with Autoregressive Diffusion
en.desc: Talker-T2AV introduces a new method for creating talking head videos by using an autoregressive diffusion framework. This approach separates the high-level reasoning about audio and video from the detailed adjustments needed for each modality, which helps improve lip-sync accuracy and consistency between audio and video. Unlike previous models that mix these processes too closely, Talker-T2AV uses a shared backbone for high-level features while employing specific decoders for audio and video details. The results show that this method outperforms existing models in generating more coherent and high-quality talking head videos.

zh.title: Talker-T2AV：提升说话者合成的跨模态一致性
zh.desc: Talker-T2AV提出了一种自回归扩散框架，用于合成说话者的头部图像，能够将高层次的跨模态推理与低层次的特定模态细化分开，从而提高了嘴唇同步的准确性和跨模态的一致性。现有的模型在去噪过程中通过广泛的注意力机制将模态耦合在一起，这种方式在处理高层语义和低层细节时并不理想。Talker-T2AV通过共享的主干网络进行高层次的跨模态建模，而低层次的细化则使用特定模态的解码器，从而避免了不必要的耦合，提高了效率。实验结果表明，Talker-T2AV在嘴唇同步准确性、视频质量和音频质量方面均优于双分支基线，展现出比级联管道更强的跨模态一致性。

papers.title: AnalogRetriever: Learning Cross-Modal Representations for Analog Circuit Retrieval
papers.abstract: Abstract AnalogRetriever is a unified tri-modal retrieval framework that enhances analog circuit design by encoding schematics, descriptions, and netlists into a shared embedding space using vision-language models and graph convolutional networks.  AI-generated summary Analog circuit design relies heavily on reusing existing intellectual property (IP), yet searching across heterogeneous representations such as SPICE netlists, schematics, and functional descriptions remains challenging. Existing methods are largely limited to exact matching within a single modality, failing to capture cross-modal semantic relationships. To bridge this gap, we present AnalogRetriever, a unified tri-modal retrieval framework for analog circuit search. We first build a high-quality dataset on top of Masala-CHAI through a two-stage repair pipeline that raises the netlist compile rate from 22\% to 100\%. Built on this foundation, AnalogRetriever encodes schematics and descriptions with a vision-language model and netlists with a port-aware relational graph convolutional network, mapping all three modalities into a shared embedding space via curriculum contrastive learning. Experiments show that AnalogRetriever achieves an average Recall@1 of 75.2\% across all six cross-modal retrieval directions, significantly outperforming existing baselines. When integrated into the AnalogCoder agentic framework as a retrieval-augmented generation module, it consistently improves functional pass rates and enables previously unsolved tasks to be completed. Our code and dataset will be released.
affiliations: Nanjing University of Posts and Telecommunications

ru.title: Кросс-модальный поиск аналоговых схем через мультимодальные встраивания
ru.desc: AnalogRetriever представляет собой унифицированную фреймворк для поиска аналоговых схем, которая кодирует принципиальные схемы, текстовые описания и сетевые списки в общее пространство встраиваний. Система использует vision-language модели для обработки визуальной информации и графовые свёрточные сети для анализа топологии цепей. Обучение проводится с применением curriculum contrastive learning, что позволяет модели найти семантические связи между разными модальностями данных. Фреймворк достигает высокую точность поиска (75.2% Recall@1) и улучшает производительность когда интегрируется в генеративные системы с извлечением контекста.

en.title: Bridging Modalities for Enhanced Analog Circuit Retrieval
en.desc: AnalogRetriever is a tri-modal retrieval framework designed to improve analog circuit design by integrating different types of data such as schematics, descriptions, and netlists into a common embedding space. It utilizes advanced techniques like vision-language models and graph convolutional networks to effectively encode these diverse representations. The framework addresses the limitations of existing methods that only perform exact matching within a single modality, enabling better cross-modal semantic understanding. Experimental results demonstrate that AnalogRetriever significantly enhances retrieval performance and functional pass rates when used in conjunction with the AnalogCoder framework.

zh.title: 统一三模态检索，提升模拟电路设计
zh.desc: AnalogRetriever 是一个统一的三模态检索框架，旨在通过使用视觉语言模型和图卷积网络，将电路图、描述和网表编码到共享的嵌入空间中，从而增强模拟电路设计。该框架解决了在不同表示（如 SPICE 网表、电路图和功能描述）之间进行检索的挑战，克服了现有方法仅限于单一模态的精确匹配的问题。通过构建高质量的数据集并采用课程对比学习，AnalogRetriever 能够有效地捕捉跨模态的语义关系。实验结果表明，该框架在六个跨模态检索方向上平均 Recall@1 达到 75.2%，显著优于现有基线。
